    Home › Shipyards › Chantiers de l'Atlantique

    Chantiers de l'Atlantique

    France · Founded 1861

    Saint-Nazaire's giant-maker — from Normandie and the half-million-tonne tankers to the Oasis class.

    Ships here24
    Longest414.22 m
    Deliveries1976–2025
    Still in service22
    The yard

    The story so far

    Few yards have built more record-holders: the liner Normandie, the France, the largest supertankers ever constructed in one piece — Batillus and Pierre Guillaumat — and, in the modern era, Queen Mary 2, the Oasis-class cruise ships and MSC World Europa all came down the same Loire-estuary slipways.

    State-owned since 2018 after passing through Alstom and STX ownership, the Saint-Nazaire yard remains one of only a handful on Earth capable of a 360-metre passenger ship, and its order book runs years deep with the next generation of LNG-powered giants.

    The line-up

    Chantiers de l'Atlantique ships

    Side profiles are sized by real length overall — longer ships draw bigger. Categories are never mixed.

    The visual reference for ships. Sourced spec tables, every operator, and every giant of the sea drawn to true scale.

Port safety Ships in storm The Silhouette Quiz Methodology Matchups Icon vs Titanic Legend vs Icon Titanic vs Queen Mary 2 Seawise Giant vs Icon Ever Given vs Ever Alot Emma Mærsk vs MSC Irina Blog The six days that stopped world trade Why the biggest ships stopped growing
    About the data. Every specification is compiled from operator, builder and registry sources and cited per row. Fleet and status figures are snapshots, marked ≈ where approximate and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. Ranks never mix units — cruise ships rank by GT, container ships by TEU, tankers by DWT. All silhouettes are original works of ships.fyi, drawn to true scale. Guides may contain clearly marked affiliate links; they never change what we recommend. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
    © 2026 ships.fyi
    RecordsExplained
